title: Why Most AI Product Strategies Are Just Feature Lists
lede: The difference between "we added AI" and an actual product strategy is the same difference between a grocery list and a recipe.
excerpt: The difference between "we added AI" and an actual product strategy is the same difference between a grocery list and a recipe.

title: The Build vs. Buy Decision Framework for AI in Product
lede: A decision framework for when to build your own model, when to use an API, and when the answer is neither.
excerpt: A decision framework for when to build your own model, when to use an API, and when the answer is neither.

title: Knowing When Not to Ship: AI Edition
lede: The hardest product decision in AI isn't what to build. It's what to stop building.
excerpt: The hardest product decision in AI isn't what to build. It's what to stop building.

title: The Product Manager's Blind Spot in AI
lede: We optimise for model accuracy when the actual failure mode is adoption.
excerpt: We optimise for model accuracy when the actual failure mode is adoption. The best model your users don't trust is worse than a mediocre one they do.

title: Evaluating AI Vendors: A Framework for Product Teams
lede: Most vendor evaluations test the demo. Here's a framework for testing what happens six months after the demo.
excerpt: Most vendor evaluations test the demo. Here's a framework for testing what happens six months after the demo.

title: What Your AI Roadmap Is Missing: The Adoption Problem
lede: You shipped the feature. The model works. Usage is flat.
excerpt: You shipped the feature. The model works. Usage is flat. The roadmap had a gap where the change management should have been.
